A DW alemã consultou juristas ucranianos para interpretarem a constituição, ao invés de consultar a própria constituição ucraniana. Claro que esses juristas disseram que Zelensky pode continuar na presidência. Afinal, ele é o queridinho da imprensa internacional – porque é o queridinho do governo dos Estados Unidos. Zelensky pode prender opositores, fechar canais de TV, colocar partidos na ilegalidade, perseguir sacerdotes e massacrar populações inteiras na fronteira com a Rússia, mas é um campeão da democracia. Zelensky não é Maduro ou Ortega para ser achincalhado na imprensa internacional.
Sucursal da imprensa americana, a Rede Globo obviamente também teve de sair em defesa de Zelensky. O G1 publicou uma matéria que buscava justificar em cada parágrafo a continuidade inconstitucional do presidente ucraniano, enquanto o jornal O Globo afirmou que o questionamento da legitimidade de Zelensky não passa de “propaganda de guerra” da Rússia.
O ângulo unidirecional na cobertura da imprensa não é um acidente. A pauta foi entregue, como sempre, pelo Departamento de Estado dos EUA, com a seguinte orientação do secretário Antony Blinken, no começo do mês: as eleições ocorrerão “quando os ucranianos concordarem que as condições permitem sua realização”. Todos que entenderam o recado sabem que Blinken quis dizer “quando 
Não é somente pela não realização das eleições de março deste ano que Zelensky é um presidente ilegítimo. Todo presidente ucraniano desde 2014, quando Viktor Yanukovich foi derrubado por um golpe de extrema-direita, legal e constitucionalmente é um presidente ilegítimo. O regime nascido do golpe de 2014 é uma ditadura e o presidente é escolhido entre os administradores dessa ditadura, sem verdadeira oposição. Mas Zelensky parece estar inaugurando um novo capítulo na história da ditadura ucraniana: ao romper com a formalidade eleitoral, ele tenta se perpetuar no poder. Isso certamente causará discórdia com outros setores do regime, que querem sacá-lo do poder sem modificar a estrutura ditatorial deste regime.
Mas como Zelensky é o queridinho dos Estados Unidos, da União Europeia e da OTAN, e como estes são quem realmente comanda a ditadura ucraniana, ele deverá ter algum sucesso ao menos momentâneo. O imperialismo precisa manter Zelensky à cabeça do governo, do contrário uma mudança poderia desestabilizar ainda mais o regime, o que afetaria o desempenho do exército na linha de frente e poderia antecipar uma derrota militar para a Rússia. E a derrota para a Rússia seria o pior dos pesadelos do imperialismo, que já deixou pública a sua disposição de lutar “até o último ucraniano”.
en_title: What's left to call Zelensky a dictator?
en_content:
Vladimir Zelensky was elected President of Ukraine in 2019 promising the end of hostilities against the rebel regions of the country and the normalization of relations with Russia. Ukraine came from five years of civil war, triggered by sending army troops against the populations of Donetsk and Lugansk after they rebelled against the coup d'état which overthrew an elected president and imposed a dictatorship in the country.
Those two regions, of a Russian ethnic majority, had been the most affected by the coup. Viktor Yanukovich, the overthrown president, had received most of the votes in the Donbass, in southeastern Ukraine, where much of the population is Russian. One of the first measures of the new regime was to ban the Russian language of the educational system: before ethnic Russians had the right to be educated in their own language, they were now imposed a foreign language to them. The broadcast of radio and TV programming in Russian language was prohibited. Demonstrations were called with slogans like “Yanukovich is our president!”. To suppress discontent, the new regime sent troops accompanied by neo-Nazi paramilitaries, causing massacres like the House of Unions in May 2014 in Odessa, where about 50 opponents were burned alive. The inhabitants of Donetsk and Lugansk responded by organizing popular militias and conducting referendums where most decided to separate from Ukraine because they did not want to submit to a hostile regime that oppressed them and that had already suppressed the anti-lpista movement in Odessa and Kharkiv by force. Then the war broke.
When Zelensky rose to power, the dictatorship was already consolidated. The opposition parties, such as the Party of the Regions (the largest in the country until the coup) and the Communist Party (the second largest) could not freely organize themselves or participate in the elections. The citizens of Donbass who refused to recognize the new regime were already officially considered terrorists and those who fell prisoners were tortured and killed. Alexander Kharitonov was arrested. Lyubov Korsakova had to flee to Russia, where he was still the victim of an attack. I talked to the two in 2022. There are only two of thousands of victims of repression in Donbass. About 15,000 people died due to Kiev's aggression since 2014.
The neo-Nazi battalions, such as Azov, Aidar and Tornado, as well as extreme right-wing organizations such as the Praviy Sektor and the Svoboda, acted freely and were even incorporated into the Ukrainian state – the army and the official policy, with members in the parliament. Meanwhile, the opposition was absolutely exterminated. In 2021, three large TV channels opponents (112 Ukraina, NewsOne and ZIK) were closed. censorship affects radio and TV stations, printed newspapers, websites and social networks, including YouTube channels. Censorship is “worthy of the worst authoritarian regimes,” the European Federation of Journalists reported in 2023. The largest religious community in the country, the Ukrainian Orthodox Church, was put in illegality by parliament in October last year.
Today, in addition to the Party of the Regions and the Communist Party, the Socialist Party, Socialist Progressive Party (from Kharitonov and Korsakova), the Union of Left Forces, Socialists, Block of Opposition, Justice and Development, State, OURS and the Block of Vladimir Saldo, among others. The SBU (Ukrainian political police) justifies the bans
Despite all these dictatorial measures, censorship, persecution and imprisonment of opponents, the illegalization of political parties and religious entities and the dominance of openly Nazi forces, Ukraine is considered a democracy by imperialist leaders and the communications monopoly of the West. All these obvious violations of democratic freedoms have been concealed or at most minimized over the past ten years.
The Freedom House, organization
The most recent topic of the list of arbitrariness of the Ukrainian regime was the expiration of Zelensky's mandate on May 20 without further elections being held. The Ukrainian constitution orders them to occur every five years, and in fact they should have occurred in March this year. However, the government claims that martial law applied since February 2022 does not allow elections to be held while in force. That is why the parliamentary elections, which should have taken place in October 2023, were also postponed indefinitely.
In general, international coverage in the world's leading press outlets has shut down on this subject. The few matters that cite the expiration of Zelensky's mandate argue that martial law prevents elections. This is a forgery of Ukrainian legislation. A
In its only mention of the expiration of Zelensky's mandate, CNN exhibited a live conversation between anchor Paula Newton and the correspondent in Kiev Nataliya Gumenyuk, in which the latter tries to justify the extension of Zelensky's mandate by saying, among other things, that the constitution was written at a time when no one imagined that a war would happen. For the law, even more for the constitution of a country, what is worth is what is written. If the constitution provides for five years of presidential term and does not provide for extension of the term, because it is unconstitutional.
The German DW consulted Ukrainian jurists to interpret the constitution, rather than consulting the Ukrainian constitution itself. Of course, these jurists said Zelensky can continue in the presidency. After all, he is the darling of the international press – because it is the darling of the United States government. Zelensky can arrest opponents, close TV channels, put parties in illegality, pursue priests and massacre entire populations on the border with Russia, but is a champion of democracy. Zelensky is not Maduro or Ortega to be harbored in the international press.
American press branch, Rede Globo obviously also had to go out in defense of Zelensky. The G1 published an article that sought to justify in each paragraph the unconstitutional continuity of the Ukrainian president, while the newspaper The Globe stated that the questioning of Zelensky's legitimacy is nothing but Russia's "war propaganda".
The unidirectional angle on press coverage is not an accident. The agenda was delivered, as always, by the U.S. Department of State, with the following guidance of Secretary Antony Blinken, at the beginning of the month: the elections will take place “when the Ukrainians agree that the conditions allow their realization”. Everyone who understood the message knows that Blinken meant “when
It is not only for the failure of the March elections this year that Zelensky is an illegitimate president. Every Ukrainian president since 2014, when Viktor Yanukovich was overthrown by a far-right coup, legally and constitutionally is an illegitimate president. The regime born of the coup 2014 is a dictatorship and the president is chosen among the administrators of this dictatorship, without real opposition. But Zelensky seems to be opening a new chapter in the history of the Ukrainian dictatorship: by breaking with the electoral formality, he tries to perpetuate himself in power. This will certainly cause discord with other sectors of the regime, who want to shake it out of power without modifying the dictatorial structure of this regime.
But as Zelensky is the darling of the United States, the European Union and NATO, and since these are who really commands the Ukrainian dictatorship, he must have some success at least momentary. Imperialism needs to keep Zelensky to the head of the government, otherwise a change could further destabilize the regime, which would affect the army's performance on the front line and could anticipate a military defeat for Russia. And the defeat for Russia would be the worst of the nightmares of imperialism, which has already made public its willingness to fight “until the last Ukrainian”.
en_subtitle: The most recent topic of the list of arbitrariness of the Ukrainian regime was the expiration of Zelensky's mandate on May 20 without further elections being held

title: Brasil não terá embaixador em Israel enquanto Netanyahu seguir no poder
subtitle: Avaliação do Itamaraty é a de que o governo de Telaviv não busca um diálogo saudável com o Brasil
content:
O governo brasileiro, liderado pelo presidente Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, decidiu que não nomeará um novo embaixador para Israel até que o conflito na região seja resolvido e o primeiro-ministro Binyamin Netanyahu deixe o poder. Essa decisão reflete uma análise crítica do Itamaraty sobre a postura do governo israelense, segundo 
Segundo diplomatas brasileiros, a embaixada do Brasil em Israel encontra-se inoperante devido à guerra, sem promover atividades culturais ou comerciais, e será comandada durante este período pelo encarregado de negócios Fabio Farias.
As declarações de Lula, comparando as ações de Israel em Gaza a genocídios históricos, geraram uma forte reação do governo israelense. Isso deteriorou as relações diplomáticas, impossibilitando um diálogo construtivo entre os dois países.
Em fevereiro, o embaixador brasileiro em Israel, Frederico Meyer, foi chamado de volta ao Brasil após ser publicamente criticado por autoridades israelenses, em resposta às declarações de Lula.
O governo de Israel não se desculpou pelo incidente, o que o Itamaraty interpretou como uma tentativa de humilhação não apenas do diplomata, mas do Brasil como um todo. Pelo contrário, o chanceler israelense, Israel Katz, intensificou as críticas a Lula em suas redes sociais.
Além disso, o Brasil apoia a denúncia da África do Sul ao Tribunal Penal Internacional (TPI), acusando Israel de genocídio contra os palestinos na Faixa de Gaza, o que agrava ainda mais a tensão entre os países.
en_title: Brazil will not have ambassador in Israel while Netanyahu continue in power
en_content:
The Brazilian government, led by President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva, decided that it will not appoint a new ambassador to Israel until the conflict in the region is resolved and Prime Minister Binyamin Netanyahu leave power. This decision reflects a critical analysis of Itamaraty on the Israeli government's stance, according to
According to Brazilian diplomats, the Brazilian embassy in Israel is inoperative due to war, without promoting cultural or commercial activities, and will be commanded during this period by the businessman Fabio Farias.
Lula's statements, comparing Israel's actions in Gaza to historical genocide, generated a strong reaction from the Israeli government. This has deteriorated diplomatic relations, making constructive dialogue impossible between the two countries.
In February, the Brazilian ambassador to Israel, Frederico Meyer, was called back to Brazil after being publicly criticized by Israeli authorities in response to Lula's statements.
The Israeli government did not apologize for the incident, which Itamaraty interpreted as an attempt to humiliate not only from the diplomat, but from Brazil as a whole. On the contrary, the Israeli chancellor, Israel Katz, intensified Lula's criticism on his social networks.
In addition, Brazil supports the denunciation of South Africa to the International Criminal Court (ICT), accusing Israel of genocide against the Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, which further exacerbates the tension between the countries.
en_subtitle: Itamaraty evaluation is that the Telaviv government does not seek a healthy dialogue with Brazil

title: Grandes artistas e intelectuais querem rompimento definitivo das relações entre Brasil e Israel
subtitle: Entre os nomes que assinam a petição, destacam-se nomes como Chico Buarque, Gilberto Gil e Wagner Moura, entre outros
content:
Um grupo de renomados artistas e intelectuais, incluindo membros da comunidade judaica, enviou uma carta ao presidente Lula (PT), solicitando a ruptura das relações diplomáticas do Brasil com Israel. Eles argumentam que tal medida, sob a liderança de Lula, poderia ajudar a acabar com a "carnificina intolerável" na Faixa de Gaza.No documento, divulgado pela 
A carta é assinada por personalidades como Chico Buarque, Gilberto Gil, Wagner Moura e Emicida; escritores e intelectuais como Milton Hatoum, Raduan Nassar e Jessé Souza; advogados e juristas como Pedro Serrano, Juarez Tavares e Carol Proner; e ex-ministros como Luiz Carlos Bresser-Pereira, Paulo Sérgio Pinheiro, Eleonora Menicucci, José Dirceu e Eugênio Aragão. Judeus proeminentes como Anita Leocádia, o jornalista Breno Altman e o professor Bruno Huberman também endossam o manifesto.
Leia, abaixo, a íntegra do documento:
Carta aberta ao presidente Lula sobre o genocídio do povo palestino
Estimado presidente Lula,
Primeiramente, gostaríamos de saudá-lo por sua postura sempre firme e coerente em solidariedade ao povo palestino, denunciando constantemente o genocídio do qual são vítimas, especialmente mulheres e crianças.
O Brasil tem apresentado várias propostas para o cessar-fogo na Faixa de Gaza e para a solução de dois Estados conforme resoluções internacionais. Graças ao seu governo, somos uma das nações que reconhecem, no âmbito das Nações Unidas, a soberania e a independência da Palestina.
No entanto, a crescente violência imposta pelo governo Netanyahu, com ataques desumanos e cruéis contra civis, exige que o mundo vá além de gestos e propostas diplomáticas, como já discutem diversos países da União Europeia e outras regiões. O governo Netanyahu viola abertamente deliberações da Corte Internacional de Justiça, desrespeitando o Conselho de Segurança e a Assembleia Geral da ONU. Recentes ataques contra um acampamento de deslocados em Rafah, no sul de Gaza, com dezenas de inocentes assassinados, mostram um claro e inaceitável desprezo pela ética humanitária.
Estamos convencidos, querido presidente, que é hora de nosso país se unir às demais nações que romperam relações diplomáticas e comerciais com o Estado de Israel, exigindo o cumprimento das decisões que ponham fim ao genocídio e garantam a autodeterminação do povo palestino. Essas medidas, adotadas por nosso país sob sua liderança, certamente serviriam de exemplo a outros governos e seriam uma imensa contribuição para que se encerre essa carnificina intolerável.

en_title: Great artists and intellectuals want a definitive break in the relations between Brazil and Israel
en_content:
A group of renowned artists and intellectuals, including members of the Jewish community, sent a letter to President Lula (PT), requesting the rupture of Brazil's diplomatic relations with Israel. They argue that such a measure, under the leadership of Lula, could help to end the "inlerable carnage" in the Gaza Strip. In the document, released by
The letter is signed by personalities such as Chico Buarque, Gilberto Gil, Wagner Moura and Emicida; writers and intellectuals such as Milton Hatoum, Raduan Nassar and Jesse Souza; lawyers and jurists such as Pedro Serrano, Juarez Tavares and Carol Proner; and former ministers like Luiz Carlos Bresser-Pereira, Paulo Sérgio Pinheiro, Eleonora Menicucci, José Dirceu e Eurceu. Prominent Jews like Anita Leocadia, journalist Breno Altman and professor Bruno Huberman also endorse the manifesto.
Read, below, the entire document:
Letter open to President Lula on the genocide of the Palestinian people
Dear President Lula,
First, we would like to greet you for your always firm and consistent stance in solidarity with the Palestinian people, constantly denouncing the genocide of which they are victims, especially women and children.
Brazil has presented several proposals for the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and for the solution of two states according to international resolutions. Thanks to its government, we are one of the nations that recognize, within the United Nations, the sovereignty and independence of Palestine.
However, the growing violence imposed by the Netanyahu government, with inhuman and cruel attacks on civilians, demands that the world go beyond diplomatic gestures and proposals, as already discussed by several European Union countries and other regions. The Netanyahu government openly violates deliberations of the International Court of Justice, disrespecting the Security Council and the UN General Assembly. Recent attacks against a displaced camp in Rafah, South Gaza, with dozens of innocent people murdered, show a clear and unacceptable contempt for humanitarian ethics.
We are convinced, dear President, that it is time for our country to join the other nations that have broken diplomatic and commercial relations with the State of Israel, demanding the fulfilment of the decisions that put an end to genocide and guarantee the self-determination of the Palestinian people. These measures, adopted by our country under its leadership, would certainly serve as an example to other governments and would be an immense contribution to ending this intolerable carnage.
